title: [Feature Request] Android TV

body: Have you thought about making an Android TV branch? This looks great as mobile, I'd love to see what you could do with a tv interface!

title: Migrate to MoviebaseApp/tmdb-api
author: ChrisKruegerDev
body:
Hi @JoelKanyi, you have a great movie app, and I am looking to contribute with a TMDB API for Kotlin Multiplatform.

The project fully covers TMDB API, with everything tested for Android projects. So you save a lot of work, and you can focus on the UI layer 🙂

https://github.com/MoviebaseApp/tmdb-api

title: Vertically scrollable component was measured with an infinity maximum height constraints
author: tenSunFree
body: Vertically scrollable component was measured with an infinity maximum height constraints, which is disallowed. One of the common reasons is nesting layouts like LazyColumn and Column(Modifier.verticalScroll()). You can try removing verticalScroll(), because LazyColumn already provides scrolling.

title: Add an Expandable Text for film description
author: Ericgacoki
body:
```diff
+ Feature suggestion
```

![see more](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/54077752/159119813-d64b7430-732c-4ee9-8429-9a2e490878fd.png)

Adding a Expandable text will give enough room to display other composables below the description and only expand (show full text) when **"...See more"** is clicked!

title: BottomNav hides the bottom part of the Top rated movies list
author: Ericgacoki
body:
![bottom nav](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/54077752/159111841-ae174f2c-b058-4ae6-b6c8-f43d0743077a.png)

This is the maximum you can scroll to. It would be better to display the whole item and leave a space between the list and the bottom nav!

`possible fix:`
- Play around with the offsets of the parent container upon reaching the last item in the nested scroll or
- Add an empty composable, preferably a Spacer as the last item in the LazyColumn 😂 (i'd go with this one)

title: Theme violation
author: Ericgacoki
body:
![design violation](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/54077752/159111647-04d87922-1798-41a7-9113-efc055c901ce.png)

The app seems to be using dark mode and dark mode only but the "Get in touch dialog" somehow uses light mode 😎

title: App crashes upon clicking the favorite icon
author: Ericgacoki
body:
```diff
- Bug report 🐛
```
![app crash](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/54077752/159111126-561410d1-c2b5-4681-bdff-1292f6e72502.png)

`desc:`

The movie details are loaded and displayed quite well. However, when you try to add the movie to your favorites, the app crashes.
Still on that, there's no live update on the icon. Users  would expect to see a different color immediately after clicking the icon - not after refreshing the screen!

`possible fix:`
-  Use Livedata or Flows in conjunction with Coroutines to capture and update the icon state at real time!
